samples.xml_description:
Implement an asynchronous FIFO with a depth of 16 and a width of 8 bits, using two separate FIFO controllers: one for writing data and another for reading data. Write clock signal. Read clock signal. Write reset signal. Read reset signal. Write increment signal. Read increment signal. Write data input. Write full signal. Read empty signal. Read data output. Width of the FIFO. Depth of the FIFO. Write address binary register. Read address binary register. Write pointer buffer. Read pointer buffer. Write pointer. Read pointer. Write full register. Read empty register.

Instantiate a dual-port RAM module with the specified depth and width for data storage.

Write pointer update logic triggered on the positive edge of the write clock or the negative edge of the write reset.

If the write increment signal is high, increment the write address binary register.

Gray code conversion of the write pointer.

Buffering the write pointer for synchronization.

Read pointer update logic triggered on the positive edge of the read clock or the negative edge of the read reset.

If the read increment signal is high, increment the read address binary register.

Gray code conversion of the read pointer.

Buffering the read pointer for synchronization.

Full signal generation logic based on the comparison of the write pointer and the synchronized read pointer.

Empty signal generation logic based on the comparison of the read pointer and the synchronized write pointer.

Input and output signal connections with the dual-port RAM module.
